commit.message:
Handle openid-vc urls in webview, Fixes AB#3535641 (#3013)

**Context** : Total loss recovery is a feature introduced by VID team to
recover an account. This feature will be triggered from inside of a
webview when the user clicks on "Recover your account" link. After this
a 3P identity verifier will verify the user and provide a credential.
Once a credential is available, a specific redirect URI is triggered
**openid-vc://**? request_uri=https://verifier.example.com/request/123,
which will invoke wallet library in Authenticator app --- All this is
functionality not connected to broker except the fact that the URLs are
all being loaded in our webview.

**What is openid-vc** ? : The openid-vc:// scheme is part of the
OpenID4VC family of specifications, which includes:
OpenID4VCI (Verifiable Credential Issuance) — how a wallet receives
credentials from an issuer
OpenID4VP (Verifiable Presentations) — how a wallet presents credentials
to a verifier

**Problem** : When a web page loaded in the authentication WebView
navigates to an openid-vc:// URI (used by OpenID Verifiable Credentials
flows), the WebView cannot handle this non-HTTP custom scheme natively.
The navigation fails, blocking the VC issuance/presentation flow.

**Solution** : Intercept **openid-vc://** navigations in
AzureActiveDirectoryWebViewClient.handleUrl() and delegate them to the
Android system so a registered wallet app can handle the request.

Fixes
[AB#3535641](https://identitydivision.visualstudio.com/fac9d424-53d2-45c0-91b5-ef6ba7a6bf26/_workitems/edit/3535641)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ghostty): pre-warm portal.Desktop and Flatpak synchronously

The previous wrapper fired StartServiceByName for org.freedesktop.Flatpak
in the background (&), which raced with ghostty's own D-Bus calls — ghostty
could still stall waiting for a cold service.

Change to synchronous pre-warm of both:
  - org.freedesktop.portal.Desktop  (xdg-desktop-portal, used for
    GlobalShortcuts and portal settings on every startup)
  - org.freedesktop.Flatpak         (flatpak-session-helper, used for
    FlatpakHostCommand to spawn shells on the host)

When both services are already running each gdbus call returns in <10 ms.
When cold they block for the activation duration (~1-3 s each) so ghostty
starts with both services provably ready, eliminating any internal timeout.

Also update GOTCHAS.md with corrected dual-service root cause, improved
diagnostics, and a note about the host-level portal backend conflict case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(metrics): add cross-CLI token usage tracking and dashboard enhancements (#16)

# Pull request description

## Describe your changes

- **New `CLIUsageProvider`**: reads local session data from GitHub
Copilot (`~/.copilot/session-state/*/events.jsonl`), Gemini CLI
(`~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/session-*.json`), and Claude Code
(`~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl`) to surface real per-CLI token
consumption alongside RTK savings.
- **Dashboard redesign**: replaced the two old charts (doughnut + pie)
with a single donut chart showing RTK Saved + per-CLI token slices, and
a taller multi-line daily chart with one line per CLI plus RTK
input/output/saved.
- **New summary cards**: CLI Tool Calls (Copilot
`tool.execution_complete`, Gemini `toolCalls`, Claude `tool_use`
blocks), CLI Total (with per-CLI sublabel), and True Savings % (`saved /
(CLI total + RTK input)`).
- **Gemini token inflation fix**: uses `output + thoughts` per message
instead of `total`, avoiding ~300× overcounting from cumulative context
re-sent each turn.
- **Overlap filtering**: CLI tokens are only counted on days where RTK
also has data for an apples-to-apples savings comparison.
- **Removed status bar item** and all associated polling code from
`extension.ts`.
- **README updated** with dashboard screenshot, supported CLIs table,
and revised feature descriptions.

## Issue ticket number and link

## Checklist before requesting a review

- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published in downstream
modules
- [ ] New or updated tests follow the AAA pattern and
`test_<feature>_should_<expected behavior>_when_<state under test>`
naming convention

fix: resilient usage display under API rate limiting (#193)

* fix: resilient usage display under API rate limiting

The Anthropic usage API rate-limits to ~1 call per 5 minutes. With the
previous 60s cache TTL, 4 out of 5 API calls returned 429, causing the
HUD to permanently display "(429)" instead of actual usage data.

Three-layer fix:
- Increase cache TTL from 60s to 5 minutes to match rate limit window
- Preserve lastGoodData in cache across rate-limited periods so the HUD
  always shows the best available data instead of errors
- Exponential backoff (60s→120s→240s→5min cap) with Retry-After header
  support for consecutive 429 responses

Also show "syncing..." instead of raw HTTP status on first-run rate limit.

* Update usage-api.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rename misleading input_rank param to input_dim_value in processSlice… (#7739)

This pull request improves the handling of the `Slice` operator when
applied to empty or scalar-shaped tensors, ensuring correct shape
inference and preventing potential crashes. The changes include both a
bug fix in the shape inference logic and the addition of a targeted test
case.

**Bug fix for `Slice` shape inference:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to use the
actual input dimension size or value (instead of input rank) when
calculating slice bounds, ensuring correct handling for empty or scalar
dimensions.

**Testing improvements:**

* Added a new test `test_slice_scalar_shape_output` in
`shape_inference_test.py` to verify that slicing the shape of a scalar
tensor produces a zero-length output and does not crash.

commit.message:
fix: defensive truncation gates prevent UBSAN on truncated profiles

iccanalyzer-lite links UNPATCHED iccDEV and must handle all security
findings through defensive programming — never library patches.

Truncated profiles (header.size > actual file size) cause UBSAN when
CIccProfile::Read() eagerly loads tags whose data lies beyond EOF.
Invalid enum values in calculator elements trigger CWE-681.

Three truncation gates added:
1. IccAnalyzerSecurity.cpp: skipLibraryPhase when H1 detects truncation
2. IccAnalyzerValidation.cpp: RoundTripAnalyze() skips Read() on truncation
3. IccAnalyzerComprehensive.cpp: phases 3-5 skip on truncation

IsProfileTruncated() helper reads first 4 bytes (BE profile size) and
compares against stat() file size — lightweight, no library dependency.

Verified: 230/230 tests pass, 0 ASAN/UBSAN on crash PoCs including
AFL-found truncated v5 spac profile (2267 vs 3928 bytes).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ghostty): pre-warm D-Bus portal to eliminate 10s first-launch delay

Ghostty is built with -Dflatpak=true, which enables an internal D-Bus
bridge to org.freedesktop.Flatpak for host-process spawning. On first
launch after boot, if flatpak-session-helper is not yet running, D-Bus
must cold-start it synchronously — taking ~10 seconds.

Add ghostty-wrapper.sh as the Flatpak command. It fires a non-blocking
gdbus StartServiceByName call for org.freedesktop.Flatpak in the
background before exec-ing ghostty, giving the portal service a head
start so it is ready by the time ghostty needs it.

If the delay persists after this fix, it is likely GTK4 shader
compilation; test with GSK_RENDERER=cairo and add --env=GSK_RENDERER=gl
to finish-args if confirmed. Diagnosis commands documented in GOTCHAS.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[CI] Add manifest diff workflow (Part 2/3) (#3301)

## Motivation

Add a reusable GitHub Actions workflow for generating manifest diff
reports on-demand. This workflow enables both manual triggering and
integration with other CI workflows.

[**Example
Report**](https://therock-ci-artifacts.s3.amazonaws.com/23025588012-linux/logs/manifest-diff/TheRockReport.html)

This is Part 2/3 of the manifest diff report feature (split from #2161).
- Part 1: #3295 - Python scripts and tests
- Part 2: This PR - Reusable workflow
- Part 3: CI nightly integration (depends on this PR)

## Technical details

- **`.github/workflows/manifest-diff.yml`**
  - `workflow_dispatch` for manual runs (GitHub UI or CLI).
  - `workflow_call` for use by other workflows (e.g. `ci_nightly.yml`).
- Single “Generate manifest diff report” step: all inputs are passed
through to the script; the script validates combinations and reports
clear errors for invalid input.
- Supports the same four input combinations (see below); validation and
behavior live in the script, not in workflow conditionals.
- S3 upload of the generated HTML report via
`upload_test_report_script.py` (when not from a fork).

- **`build_tools/github_actions/upload_test_report_script.py`**
- Appends the S3 report URL to the job step summary via
`gha_append_step_summary` so the report link appears in the Actions UI.
Safe when not in GHA (no-op if `GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` is unset).

- **Script and template (supporting changes)**  
- Script: optional args from the workflow (e.g. empty `--start` /
`--find-last-successful`) are normalized and validated so invalid
combinations fail with a clear message.
- Template: commit range arrow and section icons use HTML entities and
`<meta charset="utf-8">` so the report displays correctly and avoids
encoding issues (e.g. mojibake in the commit range line).


Input combinations supported:
- `find_last_successful` + `workflow_mode`: Find start from last
successful, end as workflow ID
- `find_last_successful` only: Find start from last successful, end as
commit SHA
- `workflow_mode` only: Both start and end as workflow IDs
- Neither: Both start and end as commit SHAs

## Test Plan

- [x] Trigger workflow manually with
`find_last_successful=ci_nightly.yml` and hardcoded parameters
- [x] Verify report is generated and uploaded to S3
- [x] Verify step summary appears in GitHub Actions UI

## Test Result

Test Case 1: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 1 (find-last-successful +
workflow
mode)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024165253/job/66867809624#step:3:2)
Test Case 2: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 2 (find-last-successful, end
as
SHA)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024353822/job/66868457526#step:3:2)
Test Case 3: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 3 (workflow mode, start/end
as run
IDs)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024506197/job/66868997210#step:3:2)
Test Case 4: [[CI] Manifest-diff: Test Case 4 (start/end as commit
SHAs)](https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/actions/runs/23024684367/job/66869608549#step:3:2)

## Submission Checklist

- [ ] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.
